title: MCP Security, Prompt Injection, and Supply Chain Trust
description: Threat-model an MCP server consuming untrusted content and exposing write tools. Learners cumulatively produce an MCP security test suite and operating policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Inspect server provenance, dependencies, update channel, permissions, and data destinations.
Treat external resource and tool content as untrusted data that cannot redefine authority.
Bind actions to user intent, authorization, audience, and protected resource.
